question: Has Boeing reported any materially important ongoing legal battles from FY2022?
gold_answer: Yes. Multiple lawsuits have been filed against Boeing resulting from a 2018 Lion Air crash and a 2019 Ethiopian Airlines crash.
predicted: Yes, Boeing reported several materially important ongoing legal battles from FY2022. According to Note 21 – Legal Proceedings: (1) Multiple legal actions related to the Lion Air Flight 610 (October 2018) and Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 (March 2019) accidents. Boeing entered into a Deferred Prosecution Agreement with the U.S. Department of Justice in 2021 regarding the 737 MAX evaluation. In September 2022, Boeing settled an SEC investigation and consented to a $200 million civil penalty, which was paid in October 2022. The company received $219 million from a shareholder derivative lawsuit settlement in Q2 2022. Boeing states it cannot reasonably estimate remaining losses from pending lawsuits related to these accidents. (2) A dispute with Embraer S.A. over the termination of joint venture agreements valued at $4.2 billion, which is currently in arbitration and expected to be completed in late 2023 or early 2024. Boeing cannot reasonably estimate the range of loss from this arbitration.
reasoning: Now I have the actual page text from pages 112-113. Let me look for information about Boeing's material legal proceedings from FY2022. Page 113 contains Note 21 - Legal Proceedings which discusses the ongoing legal matters.
label: AL
